identity: Nolly (Adam 'Nolly' Getgood of Periphery) — designed for modern progressive metal and djent. Known for surgical EQ precision with separate Low Mid and High Mid controls, deep tight low end, and extremely controlled gain response. Best for: djent, prog metal, technical metal.

unique_controls_explained.Low Mid: Controls the 200–800Hz region. Boosting adds body and thickness; cutting scoops the mid-range for a more open, modern sound. This is a NOLLY-SPECIFIC control not found on most other NDSP plugins.
unique_controls_explained.High Mid: Controls the 800Hz–3kHz region. Boosting adds presence and bite; cutting tames harshness. Also NOLLY-SPECIFIC. Together with Low Mid, this gives far more EQ precision than a single Mid knob.
unique_controls_explained.Input Gain: Pre-amp gain stage. Set this before dialing the amp gain to control how hot the signal enters the preamp.
unique_controls_explained.Boost: Engages an internal clean boost/overdrive boost circuit — useful for lead sections or tightening the low end further.

mid_scooped.description: Classic V-shape EQ — scooped mids, boosted bass and treble. Open, wide, modern metal sound.

mid_forward.description: Pushed mids for cut in a mix. Aggressive, present tone that sits on top of the mix.

djent_tight.description: Tight, percussive low end with controlled gain. Nolly's signature djent palm-mute clarity.

warm_thick.description: Low-mid boosted for body. Warm, thick rhythm tone with organic feel.

high_gain_modern.description: Maximum modern high gain — tight bass, scooped lows/mids, bright presence.

recommended_chain_order: Compressor (optional, light — Nolly tones usually skip this)
Boost / Overdrive (e.g. Maxon OD808 or TS-style) → into Nolly Input
Post-amp EQ (subtle notch filtering if needed)

boost_logic.purpose: A TS-style overdrive (Tube Screamer / OD808) placed BEFORE the Nolly input is the classic djent boost. It tightens the low end, compresses the signal slightly, and pushes the preamp harder without adding standalone distortion.
boost_logic.boost_settings: Drive: very low (0–2). Tone: 12 o'clock. Level: high (7–9). This boosts the signal level, not the distortion.
boost_logic.when_to_use_internal_boost: Use Nolly's built-in Boost for lead sections or when you want a quick volume/gain jump without changing the preamp character significantly.

pedal_chain_logic.gate_logic: Set Threshold just above the noise floor of your guitar — typically -40 to -50 dB for active pickups, -35 to -45 dB for passives. Attack fast (5ms), Release medium (100–200ms) so notes breathe naturally.
pedal_chain_logic.cab_logic: Low Cut at 80–100Hz to remove sub-rumble. High Cut at 8–10kHz to tame harshness. Mic Position closer = more presence; further = more room. Room Amount low (0–20%) for modern tight metal.

identity: John Petrucci (Dream Theater) — designed for progressive metal with versatile clean/crunch/lead sounds. Features a Rhythm/Lead toggle for instant channel switching and covers everything from clean Dream Theater choruses to full Mesa-style high gain leads.

unique_controls_explained.Rhythm/Lead Toggle: Two-channel system — Rhythm channel is optimized for tighter, more controlled gain; Lead channel opens up for more sustain, compression, and singing high-gain tone. PETRUCCI-SPECIFIC. Behaves like a channel switch on a Mesa Boogie.
unique_controls_explained.Mid: Single mid knob (unlike Nolly's dual Low Mid/High Mid). Covers the full mid range in one control — less surgical but easier to use.
unique_controls_explained.Speaker Size: Cab voicing control — smaller speaker = tighter, more present. Larger = bigger, looser low end.
unique_controls_explained.Low Shelf / High Shelf: Cab-level EQ shelves for broad tonal shaping at the output stage, separate from the amp EQ.

mid_scooped.description: Mesa-style scoop. Wide V-shape for modern metal rhythm.

mid_forward.description: Full mids for lead cut. Petrucci lead tone character.

prog_clean.description: Clean Dream Theater chorus-style tone. Low gain, full mids, chorus applied.

high_gain_lead.description: Maximum sustain lead tone — Petrucci solo voice.

recommended_chain_order: Compressor (light — enhances clean tones, bypass for high gain)
TS-style Boost (optional, for tightening rhythm channel)
Petrucci Amp (use Rhythm/Lead Toggle for channel switching)

boost_logic.purpose: Petrucci's Mesa-style amp is already high gain. A TS boost before the amp tightens the low end. Use the internal Lead channel for leads rather than stacking a separate boost.
boost_logic.when_to_use_internal_boost: Use the Rhythm/Lead Toggle to switch between rhythm and lead. The Lead channel inherently adds more compression and sustain.
boost_logic.compressor_note: Light compressor before the amp enhances clean and crunch tones significantly. Bypass it for full high gain.

pedal_chain_logic.gate_logic: Medium threshold. Petrucci tones often have moderate amounts of natural sustain, so don't over-gate.
pedal_chain_logic.cab_logic: Speaker Size to taste — medium size (middle position) suits most prog metal. Low Shelf slight cut below 80Hz. High Shelf slight cut above 10kHz for smoothness.

identity: Gojira (Joe Duplantier) — designed for progressive death metal with massive, organic, earthy heaviness. The defining feature is the DEPTH control, which adds a unique low-frequency resonance and 'hugeness' not found in other NDSP plugins. Best for: death metal, prog metal, sludge, atmospheric heavy.

unique_controls_explained.Depth: THIS IS THE DEFINING GOJIRA CONTROL. Controls the low-frequency resonance and 'chest-thump' of the amp. Unlike Bass (which shapes mid-bass), Depth affects the sub-low resonance and power amp feel. High Depth = massive earth-shaking heaviness. Low Depth = tighter, more controlled low end. This control does NOT exist on Nolly, Petrucci, or Fortin Nameless. It emulates the power amp sag and speaker resonance of a cranked tube amp.
unique_controls_explained.Air: Cab-level control adding 'air' and high-frequency openness to the cabinet sound. More Air = brighter, more open top end. Unique to Gojira's cab section.
unique_controls_explained.Mic Blend: Blends between two mic positions/types on the cabinet — unique cab control giving more tonal options than a simple mic selector.
unique_controls_explained.Whammy: Pitch shift / whammy effect — used in Gojira for their signature harmonic whammy techniques.
unique_controls_explained.Synth: Synth effect that processes the guitar signal into synth-like tones — experimental/unconventional, used for atmospheric moments.
unique_controls_explained.Fuzz: Dedicated fuzz circuit — used for heavier saturation or Gojira's more extreme moments.

mid_scooped.description: Gojira's open, massive rhythm scoop — wide V-shape with heavy Depth.

mid_forward.description: Forward mids with depth for chunky, earthy rhythm.

earth_shaking_heavy.description: Maximum Depth and Gain for Gojira's most massive tones. Sub-low resonance cranked.

atmospheric_clean.description: Low gain atmospheric clean with Air opened up — used in Gojira's quiet sections.

tight_modern_death.description: Controlled, tight death metal tone — moderate Depth so bass doesn't overwhelm.

recommended_chain_order: Whammy (if used — place first for pitch accuracy)
Noise Gate (pre-amp, tight threshold for death metal)
Fuzz (if used — can also be placed post-amp for different character)
Reverb (post-amp — large hall/room for atmospheric moments)

boost_logic.purpose: Gojira's amp is already voiced for extreme heaviness. A boost pedal before the amp (TS-style) can tighten the Depth response and make palm mutes more percussive. However, many Gojira tones skip the boost and let the amp breathe naturally.
boost_logic.depth_interaction: When using a boost, reduce Depth slightly (0.5–1 point) to compensate — the boost adds tightness that can clash with very high Depth settings, causing muddiness.
boost_logic.whammy_placement: Always place the Whammy FIRST in chain before any dirt or amp. This ensures pitch tracking is accurate.

pedal_chain_logic.gate_logic: Tight gate for death metal rhythms. Threshold -40dB, fast attack, medium release. For atmospheric sections, reduce threshold or bypass gate entirely.
pedal_chain_logic.cab_logic: Cabinet Type: Mesa/Recto-style or similar heavy cab. Low Cut: 80–100Hz. High Cut: 9–12kHz. Air: 3–6 for natural open top end. Keep Mic Blend centered or adjust to taste — blending adds dimension.

identity: Parallax — NDSP's bass-specific plugin but also widely used for sub-drop guitar tones, 8-string/extended range guitar, and extreme modern metal. Unique two-stage architecture (Preamp + Power Amp as separate sections). Designed for tight, modern, controlled extreme-range tones.

unique_controls_explained.Contour: PARALLAX-SPECIFIC. A single knob that simultaneously scoops mids and boosts bass/treble — like a built-in Baxandall EQ curve. Clockwise = more scooped V-shape. Counter-clockwise = flat/mid-forward. Very powerful tone shaping tool not found on other NDSP plugins.
unique_controls_explained.Sag: POWER AMP section. Controls power amp sag — how the amp responds dynamically to transients. High Sag = vintage, compressed, slightly loose feel. Low Sag = tight, modern, zero-compression. For modern metal, keep Sag low.
unique_controls_explained.Bias: POWER AMP section. Controls the bias point of the virtual power tubes. Low Bias = colder, tighter, more aggressive. High Bias = warmer, more harmonically complex. PARALLAX-SPECIFIC power amp control.
unique_controls_explained.Input: Pre-preamp level control — sets how hot the signal enters before Drive. Similar to Input Gain on Nolly but on the Parallax it specifically interacts with the Contour shaping.
unique_controls_explained.Mic 2 Blend: Blends a second mic into the cab simulation — gives more 3D, layered cab response.
unique_controls_explained.Low / High: Simple 2-band EQ in the preamp (vs Nolly's more detailed 4-band). Low = bass shelf. High = treble shelf.

mid_scooped.description: Max Contour for extreme V-shape scoop. Ideal for sub-drop and 8-string rhythm.

mid_forward.description: Contour pulled back to keep mids present. Earthy, heavy but cutting.

ultra_tight_modern.description: Low Sag for zero compression, medium Contour. Maximum tightness for extended range.

vintage_sag.description: High Sag for vintage compressed amp feel. Less typical for metal but great for heavier rock.

recommended_chain_order: Boost / Overdrive (optional — Contour on Parallax often replaces need for boost)

boost_logic.purpose: Parallax's Contour knob does much of the tonal heavy lifting. A boost pedal before the Input adds more drive into the preamp, but be careful — high Contour + high boost can get muddy on low strings.
boost_logic.sag_and_boost_interaction: If using a boost pedal, set Sag low (1–3) to compensate for the extra dynamic compression the boost adds. High Sag + boost = overly mushy feel.

pedal_chain_logic.gate_logic: For extended range / sub-drop: Threshold relatively high (-35 to -45dB), Release medium-fast. Sub frequencies have more sustain decay so don't cut Release too fast.
pedal_chain_logic.cab_logic: Mic 2 Blend adds dimension — blend in a second mic at 20–40% for more realistic cab response. Room very low for modern metal (0–15%). Low Cut at 60–80Hz (lower than typical — Parallax is designed for extended range).

identity: Fortin Nameless (collaboration with Mike Fortin) — one of NDSP's most aggressive, stripped-back high gain offerings. No-nonsense extreme metal tone machine. Simple EQ, massive gain on tap, built-in noise gate, and an internal boost circuit. Best for: black metal, death metal, thrash, extreme metal.

unique_controls_explained.Boost Level: FORTIN-SPECIFIC. An internal clean boost/preamp boost circuit. Cranking Boost Level tightens the low end and adds more preamp saturation — the Fortin Nameless Boost is famous for its extremely tight, compressed character that defines modern black/death metal tones. This is more aggressive in character than Nolly's Boost.
unique_controls_explained.Gate Threshold: Integrated noise gate with a simple threshold control — less parameter control than Nolly's 3-parameter gate (Threshold/Attack/Release) but faster to set up. Built directly into the FX section.
unique_controls_explained.Distance: Mic distance in the cab section — directly controls room/proximity interaction. Closer = more presence and proximity effect. Further = more natural, roomy tone.
unique_controls_explained.Gain: Extremely high gain on tap — the Nameless is designed to run with Gain high (7–10). Unlike some NDSP amps, this one is voiced for extreme gain as default character.

typical_ranges.Gain: 0-10 (typically 7-10 for its intended extreme metal use)

mid_scooped.description: Extreme metal scoop — Fortin's signature aggressive V-shape with Boost engaged.

mid_forward.description: More forward mids for black metal / extreme metal that needs to cut through.

black_metal_cold.description: Cold, harsh, trebly black metal tone. Thin bass, sharp treble, maximum Presence.

death_metal_chunk.description: Chunky death metal — solid bass, some mids for definition, tight Boost.

recommended_chain_order: External Noise Gate (optional — Nameless has built-in gate)
TS-style Boost (optional — Nameless Boost Level is very effective on its own)
Delay (minimal for extreme metal — short slapback or none)
Reverb (minimal for extreme metal — small room or none)

boost_logic.purpose: The Nameless has a famously powerful internal Boost Level. External TS boost is often UNNECESSARY — use the Boost Level knob instead. If stacking external boost, keep it very conservative (Drive 0, Level +3–5dB only) to avoid over-saturating.
boost_logic.boost_character: Fortin's boost is tighter and more aggressive than a standard TS. It acts more like a tight preamp clipper than a smooth overdrive. This gives black/death metal its characteristic surgical tightness.

pedal_chain_logic.gate_logic: Use Gate Threshold to cut noise between notes. For black metal tremolo picking, set threshold carefully — too high will cut sustain on fast tremolo passages.
pedal_chain_logic.cab_logic: Keep Low Cut at 90–120Hz for extreme metal (tighter than average). High Cut at 7–9kHz for black metal (darker, harsher), 9–11kHz for death metal. Distance: closer mic position for more aggressive presence.

identity: STL Tonality Howard Benson — designed with legendary rock/pop-metal producer Howard Benson. Unlike the Neural DSP plugins which target extreme metal, this is voiced for POLISHED, RADIO-FRIENDLY hard rock and heavy rock. Think Three Days Grace, Halestorm, Papa Roach production tones. Features Resonance control alongside Presence for more power amp shaping.

unique_controls_explained.Resonance: STL BENSON-SPECIFIC. Controls the low-frequency resonance of the power amp — similar conceptually to Gojira's Depth but voiced differently. Resonance on Benson adds richness and warmth to the bottom end. For hard rock, moderate Resonance adds punch. Too high = flabby. Pairs with Presence to shape the entire amp's power amp character.
unique_controls_explained.Middle: Named 'Middle' not 'Mid' — this is a STL naming convention. Functionally it covers the mid-range but the Benson's Middle control has a slightly more scooped center frequency compared to a typical mid knob, giving it that radio-rock character.
unique_controls_explained.Compressor Threshold / Ratio: STL Benson has a DEDICATED COMPRESSOR section (not just a gate). This is used to add sustain and polish — very useful for pop-metal and radio rock production tones where consistency is important.
unique_controls_explained.Limiter: Output limiter — prevents clipping in the signal chain. Useful for consistent recording levels.
unique_controls_explained.Room: Cab room amount — adds ambience and a sense of the speaker in a real space.
unique_controls_explained.Reverb Type: Multiple reverb types available (Room, Hall, Plate, Spring) — more reverb versatility than NDSP plugins.

mid_scooped.description: Radio-rock V-shape. Smooth, polished, radio-friendly scoop.

mid_forward.description: Producer-style mid push for aggressive but polished hard rock.

polished_clean.description: Clean hard rock tone with Compressor engaged for sustain and polish.

hard_rock_crunch.description: Classic punchy hard rock crunch — Benson's main character tone.

radio_metal.description: Modern radio metal. High gain but polished. Compressor adds consistency.

recommended_chain_order: Compressor (external or use Benson's built-in — external pre-amp for more control)
Overdrive / Boost (TS-style for tightening crunch channel)
Delay (use built-in — Delay Mix around 20-30% for radio rock)
Reverb (use built-in — Plate or Room reverb for polished rock)

boost_logic.purpose: A TS-style boost into the Benson crunch channel polishes the high gain and gives that produced, studio quality tightness. Unlike extreme metal, the boost here is often set with slightly more Drive (2–4) for added saturation.
boost_logic.compressor_usage: The built-in Compressor is a key feature of the Benson. Use it for clean/crunch tones to add sustain. For full high gain, the compressor adds more dynamic consistency — useful for recording.

pedal_chain_logic.gate_logic: Moderate gate — hard rock tones don't need as extreme gating as death metal. Threshold around -45 to -50dB. Keep Release natural (150–250ms).
pedal_chain_logic.cab_logic: Room: 15–25% for a produced, slightly ambient room sound (more than typical metal plugins). Distance: medium. Low Cut 80Hz. High Cut 10–12kHz (brighter than extreme metal for radio-friendly top end).

identity: STL Tonality Andy James — designed with shred/neoclassical metal guitarist Andy James. Voiced for modern high-gain metal with a Lead Boost feature for solos. More aggressive than the Howard Benson plugin but with a slightly warmer British character. Best for: modern metal, neoclassical metal, shred, melodic metal.

unique_controls_explained.Lead Boost: STL ANDY JAMES-SPECIFIC. A dedicated boost circuit designed for lead playing — unlike a generic boost, the Lead Boost is voiced to enhance note separation and sustain for single-note lead lines. Similar in concept to Petrucci's Rhythm/Lead toggle but implemented as a boost level knob rather than a channel switch.
unique_controls_explained.EQ Low / EQ Mid / EQ High: STL ANDY JAMES-SPECIFIC. A post-amp 3-band EQ built directly into the FX chain. This gives additional EQ sculpting AFTER the amp simulation — more flexible than NDSP plugins which rely purely on the amp's tone stack. Use this to make final mix adjustments.
unique_controls_explained.Blend: Cab mic blend — mixes between mic positions/types for tonal variety. Similar to Parallax's Mic 2 Blend.
unique_controls_explained.Gate: Simple gate in the FX section — like Fortin Nameless, simpler than Nolly's 3-parameter gate.
unique_controls_explained.Mid: Standard single mid knob — like Petrucci and Fortin. Not dual-band like Nolly's Low Mid/High Mid.

mid_scooped.description: Modern metal scoop with post-amp EQ refinement.

mid_forward.description: Mid-forward for neoclassical lead character. Use Lead Boost for solos.

shred_lead.description: Andy James signature shred lead — Lead Boost engaged, mids and presence forward.

neoclassical_rhythm.description: Tight, defined rhythm for neoclassical metal — controlled bass, forward mids.

high_gain_modern.description: Full modern metal high gain with post-EQ sculpting.

recommended_chain_order: Noise Gate (use built-in Gate in FX section)
Post-amp EQ (use built-in EQ Low/Mid/High for final mix sculpting)
Delay (post-amp — dotted eighth or quarter note for lead lines)
Reverb (post-amp — Hall or Room for space)

boost_logic.purpose: The Lead Boost is designed to replace an external boost pedal for solos. For rhythm, use a TS-style before the amp to tighten the palm mutes. For leads, engage Lead Boost instead of stacking another pedal.
boost_logic.post_amp_eq_usage: The built-in EQ Low/Mid/High is a powerful tool that other NDSP plugins don't have. Use EQ Mid to notch out harshness (typically around 2–4kHz) after the amp, and EQ High to add final air and presence without adjusting the amp tone stack.

pedal_chain_logic.gate_logic: Use the built-in Gate in FX. For high gain metal, set Gate to tight threshold. For melodic passages, ease back to preserve note decay.
pedal_chain_logic.cab_logic: Blend: try 30–50% blend of second mic for more dimension in leads. Low Cut 80–90Hz. High Cut 10–12kHz. Mic Position: close for more attack, slightly back for smoothness in leads.

identity: General tone shaping concepts and pedal chain logic applicable across all plugins.

mid_scooped.description: V-shape EQ — bass and treble boosted, mids cut. Creates a wide, open, modern metal sound. Very common in American-style amps (Mesa Boogie, Peavey 6505). Can sound thin or hollow in isolation but sits wide in a full band mix.
mid_scooped.EQ_shape: Bass: HIGH | Mids: LOW (2-4) | Treble: HIGH | Presence: MEDIUM-HIGH
mid_scooped.tradeoff: Loses mid-range presence. Can get buried in a dense mix without careful cab EQ.

mid_forward.description: Mids boosted relative to bass and treble. Cuts through a mix. Classic British amp character (Marshall, Orange). Sounds aggressive and present.
mid_forward.EQ_shape: Bass: MEDIUM | Mids: HIGH (6-8) | Treble: MEDIUM | Presence: MEDIUM
mid_forward.typical_genres: Classic metal, thrash, hard rock, punk, NWOBHM
mid_forward.tradeoff: Can sound honky or nasal if mids pushed too far. Works great in a mix but can be fatiguing in isolation.

scooped_with_presence.description: V-shape scoop but with Presence boosted to retain top-end cut. Best of both worlds for modern metal — width without losing definition.
scooped_with_presence.EQ_shape: Bass: HIGH | Mids: LOW-MEDIUM | Treble: MEDIUM | Presence: HIGH
scooped_with_presence.typical_genres: Modern thrash, melodic death metal, metalcore

warm_dark.description: Bass and low-mids boosted, treble and presence reduced. Dark, thick, heavy. Classic doom/sludge character.
warm_dark.EQ_shape: Bass: HIGH | Mids: MEDIUM-HIGH | Treble: LOW | Presence: LOW
warm_dark.typical_genres: Doom metal, sludge, stoner rock, death metal (some)

bright_clean.description: Clean tone with boosted treble and presence. Glassy, articulate. Fender-style character.
bright_clean.EQ_shape: Bass: MEDIUM | Mids: MEDIUM | Treble: HIGH | Presence: HIGH

standard_chain_order: 3. Wah / Expression (before dirt so wah interacts with clean signal)
4. Compressor (before dirt for even dynamics, after for sustain/squish)
5. Boost / Overdrive (into amp input — tightens low end, pushes preamp)
7. Noise Gate (can also go before amp — pre-amp position cuts more noise)
8. EQ (post-amp for fine-tuning — some plugins have built-in)
11. Reverb (always last — the 'space' the entire signal lives in)

TS_style_tubescreamer.character: Warm, mid-boosting overdrive. Drive low, Level high. Tightens low end, pushes mids into preamp.
TS_style_tubescreamer.best_for: Djent, modern metal, tight palm mutes. Classic Nolly/Periphery tone weapon.

clean_boost.character: Transparent level boost with no added color. Just louder signal into amp.
clean_boost.best_for: Lead boost without tone change. Solos, volume matching.

hard_clipper.character: Aggressive, compressed clipper boost. More distortion character than TS.
hard_clipper.best_for: Black metal, extreme metal, death metal tightness.
hard_clipper.drive_setting: Drive: varies. Often used for additional gain staging.

eq_boost.character: Frequency-specific boost using a parametric or graphic EQ as boost.
eq_boost.best_for: Boosting specific frequencies before amp — e.g. boosting 1kHz for more midrange cut.

universal_pedal_chain_logic.gain_staging_principle: Keep each stage clean. High gain should come from the AMP, not stacking multiple overdriven pedals. Boost pedal should push the amp harder, not add its own distortion character. Total gain = Input level + Boost level + Amp Gain. Balance these three for best results.

cab_eq_universal.Low Cut: Always cut below 60–100Hz. Sub frequencies are mud in guitar. 80Hz is a safe standard.
cab_eq_universal.High Cut: Cut above 8–16kHz depending on genre. Bright for clean/indie. Dark for extreme metal.
cab_eq_universal.purpose: Cab EQ removes frequency content that doesn't belong to guitar and makes room for bass and cymbals in a mix.
